text_content: OWASP Top 10 Guideline: Prevent SQL Injection. Never concatenate or format raw user inputs directly into query strings. Always utilize parameterized SQL statements, SQLAlchemy bind parameters, or ORM methodologies to query databases securely.

OWASP Top 10 Guideline: Prevent Command Injection and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). Avoid running raw shell inputs using subprocess or os.system with raw strings. Sanitize inputs and validate templates to prevent dynamic scripting injections.

SOLID Architecture: Single Responsibility Principle (SRP). A class or function should have one, and only one, reason to change. Separate business logic from integration clients, routers, or representation formatting.

SOLID Architecture: Interface Segregation and Dependency Inversion. Avoid forcing clients to depend on methods they do not use. High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions.

Clean Code Standard: Proper Exception Handling. Never use bare exceptions like 'except:' or catch base exceptions 'except Exception:' silently unless logging and raising. Catch specific exceptions (e.g., KeyError, ValueError) to keep code logic clear and debuggable.
Clean Code Standard: Meaningful Names. Use clear, intention-revealing variable, class, and method names. Avoid magic numbers and obscure abbreviations. Functions should be small and do one thing.

Performance Standard: Database Connection and Query Optimization. Avoid N+1 queries. Use joinedload or selectinload in SQLAlchemy for eager loading of relationships. Ensure appropriate database indexes exist for lookup parameters.

Performance Standard: Efficient Loops and Async IO. Avoid performing block-level IO requests (like HTTP get or database queries) inside loops. Batch transactions or resolve queries concurrently using asyncio.gather.
Testing Guideline: Comprehensive Test Coverage. New business logic, models, or endpoints should include unit tests asserting edge cases, inputs validation, error handling, and expected behaviors.
